name: nanonis-tramea

metadata.name: nanonis-tramea
metadata.summary: Python package for controlling Nanonis Tramea software through python

documentation:
# Python Interface Package for Nanonis 

Official python package for the Nanonis TRAMEA Controller software.

## Usage

This package allows users of the Nanonis TRAMEA Controller software to use and control
said software through python commands.

## How to use

### Importing

import nanonis_tramea

### Initializing Connection through the socket module

import socket

connection = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)

connection.connect((IP_ADRESS_HERE, PORT_HERE))

nanonisInstance = nanonis_spm.Nanonis(connection)

NOTE : THE PORT HAS TO BE AN INTEGER AND THE IP ADRESS A STRING

### Enabling Debug Console Output

The function "returnDebugInfo()" takes an integer as an argument. 
This integer should be either 1 = on, or 0 = off. This option is off by default.

Enable by running:
nanonisInstance.returnDebugInfo(1)

### Examples

There is a collection of examples installed with the package.

The description of all the available functions can be found in the TCP Protocol Document, and hovering on the function depending on the used IDE.

IMPORTANT:
The TCP Interface requires every argument to be of certain size (see documentation).
This is why the Numpy dependency is required, since it enables the specification
of variable sizes. 

## Change Log

### 1.0.3
Added the function Util.VersionGet to get the software version, the MCVA5 preamplifier functions, and the V5e Generic PI Controller functions.

modules.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_CustomUOSweep
documentation: This example performs a custom user output (UO) sweep. You can create an array of
UO voltages and it will record the selected channels at each UO voltage.

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_CustomUOSweep.Sweep_UO_Index
documentation: index of UO to sweep (User Output 1)

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_CustomUOSweep.Sweep_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after sweeping to a point

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_CustomUOSweep.Sweep_AcqSignals
documentation: signals to acquire, Uset Input 1 & User Output 1

modules.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_1DSweeper
documentation: This example performs a 2D sweep using a User output and the 1D Sweeper.
For each value of the User output (y axis)
it will perform a 1D Sweep (x axis).

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_1DSweeper.Sweep_X_InitSettling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_1DSweeper.Sweep_X_Settling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_1DSweeper.Sweep_X_Period
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_1DSweeper.Sweep_X_MaxSlewRate
documentation: units/second

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_1DSweeper.Sweep_NrAcqChannels
documentation: the swept channel is part of the acquired data

modules.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_LockInSweep
documentation: This example performs a sweep of the selected Lock-In (LI) Frequency Generator.
It creates an array of frequency values within a certain range, it waits Settling Time,
and it acquires and averages the selected channels.

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_LI_Index
documentation: index of LI Freq. Gen. to sweep

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_InitSettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait before starting the sweep

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Averaging
documentation: number of samples to average

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after sweeping to each point

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_AcqSignals
documentation: signals to acquire, LI Demod. 1 X&Y

modules.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_SimpleSignalAcquisition
documentation: This example acquires one channel (i.e. output 7) with
a defined oversampling and logs the oversample value
in a variable for as many samples as defined or until
manually stopped (pressing the Spacebar)

variables.name: nanonis_tramea_examples.Example_SimpleSignalAcquisition.Channel_Index
documentation: output 7
